summary: Complete the unfinished runtime/source/provider decoupling from the prior source-decoupling goal and harden the gates that missed the violations.
rationale: The previous source-decoupling goal left concrete CTS/Liepin runtime execution paths and verification blind spots. This corrective pass keeps source_contracts thin, moves concrete source behavior to adapters, proves fixture-source execution through WorkflowRuntime, and repairs Tach/source-boundary gates.
line_count_rationale: This corrective refactor updates legacy tests and model compatibility aliases around neutral runtime contracts; the provider client receives only protocol runtime-check metadata, and the production source_contracts split stays below the per-file limit.
hard_acceptance: runtime does not import concrete seektalent.sources.cts or seektalent.sources.liepin implementation modules
runtime does not import seektalent.source_adapters implementation modules
neutral source contracts and registry are split from concrete source adapters
runtime does not contain CTS/Liepin source whitelists or concrete source execution branches
fixture_source executes through a full WorkflowRuntime round path without runtime code changes
source boundary checker catches the original missed violations
tach.toml does not permit runtime/sources/providers cycles
existing CTS and Liepin behavior remains covered and passing
verification: uv run ruff check src tests tools
uv run ty check src tests tools
risks: The current source-boundary checker was previously green despite real runtime/source coupling; this pass strengthens it against imports, source literals, dispatch maps, membership checks, mapping indexes, source defaults, and source-specific runtime names.
The previous fixture source tests bypassed WorkflowRuntime; the corrective fixture test covers the full registry-driven runtime path.
Tach previously accepted cyclic runtime/source/provider dependencies; this pass repairs the model instead of adding accepted cycles.
If source_contracts starts carrying orchestration, provider calls, source-specific budgets, query rules, or reason-code rules, stop and move that behavior back to adapters/runtime.
